premise: At 62 I’d bought a Florida condo outright with $160,000 from decades as a home health aide, and my stepson, a real estate agent, offered to manage a reverse mortgage for upgrades — he forged a power of attorney, drained the equity, sold the condo for $220,000, and told everyone I’d gambled it away. I traced the notary fraud, got his license revoked, and when the court ordered restitution, he had to auction his late mother’s diamond jewelry, where I bought the entire collection for $28,000, resold it for $130,000, and bought a better condo two doors down from the one he sold.

thumb.label: Thumbnail + metadata
thumb.detail: Stepson Stole My Condo And Called Me A Gambler. So I Watched Him Auction His Mom
